notes: Covers salaried firefighters, volunteer firefighters, and Department of Emergency Management hazardous materials officers. Rebuttable by preponderance of competent evidence to the contrary.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Rebuttable by preponderance of competent evidence to the contrary.
Covers salaried and volunteer firefighters. Broad law enforcement coverage under same section (see law_enforcement row). Rebuttable presumption.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers State Police Officers' Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputy sheriffs; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police; Capitol Police; Virginia ABC special agents; airport authority police (Metropolitan Washington, Norfolk); Virginia Port Authority police; campus police. Rebuttable presumption.
Covers salaried/volunteer EMS personnel where the locality has adopted an authorizing resolution. Coverage is not automatic — requires local legislative action. Rebuttable presumption.

condition_specific: leukemia, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, throat (pharynx/larynx/adenoid/tonsil/esophagus/trachea/nasopharynx/oropharynx/hypopharynx), ovarian, breast, colon (dx on/after 7/1/2020), brain (dx on/after 7/1/2020), testicular (dx on/after 7/1/2020), bladder (dx on/after 7/1/2023), thyroid (dx on/after 7/1/2023)
notes: Covers salaried/volunteer firefighters and DEM hazmat officers. Colon, brain, and testicular: no presumption for diagnosis before July 1, 2020. Bladder and thyroid: no presumption for diagnosis before July 1, 2023. Rebuttable presumption.

condition_specific: leukemia, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, throat, ovarian, breast, colon, brain, testicular, bladder, thyroid
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Same effective-date restrictions as salaried firefighters for colon, brain, testicular, bladder, thyroid. Rebuttable presumption.

condition_specific: leukemia, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, throat, ovarian, breast, colon, brain, testicular, bladder, thyroid
notes: Covers State Police Officers' Retirement System members who handle hazardous materials, infectious substances, radiological agents, fentanyl, or methamphetamine; commercial vehicle enforcement officers/motor carrier safety troopers; arson/bomb investigators (state police); full-time enforcement division motor vehicle officers. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: 6-month waiting period post-hiring applies unless there is documented exposure within 6 months.
notes: Covers salaried/volunteer firefighters. Requires documented occupational exposure to blood or body fluids. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: 6-month waiting period applies unless there is documented exposure within 6 months.
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: 6-month waiting period applies unless there is documented exposure within 6 months.
notes: Covers salaried/volunteer EMS personnel. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: 6-month waiting period applies unless there is documented exposure within 6 months.
notes: Covers State Police Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputies; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police; Capitol Police; ABC special agents; airport authority police; Virginia Port Authority police; campus police. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: 6-month waiting period applies unless there is documented exposure within 6 months.
notes: Correctional officers covered. Presumption does not apply for diagnosis before July 1, 2020. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: Applies to deaths/disabilities on or after July 1, 2020.
notes: Salaried/volunteer firefighters. Requires positive diagnostic test, appropriate incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies from presumption. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: Applies to deaths/disabilities on or after July 1, 2020.
notes: Law enforcement officers as defined in § 9.1-101. Requires positive diagnostic test, incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: Applies to deaths/disabilities on or after July 1, 2020.
notes: Correctional officers and regional jail officers. Requires positive diagnostic test, incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies. Rebuttable presumption.

filing_deadline_notes: Benefits capped at 52 weeks from diagnosis date. No benefits beyond 4 years from qualifying event.
notes: Covers salaried firefighters (including special forest wardens, EMS personnel, fire scene investigators) and volunteer firefighters/EMS. Requires diagnosis by board-certified psychiatrist or licensed psychologist. Qualifying events: serious bodily injury/death, incidents involving minors, immediate life threat, mass casualties, or crime scene investigation on or after July 1, 2020. Excludes PTSD from personnel actions. Not a true rebuttable presumption — requires diagnosis + qualifying event criteria (no presumption of causation, just compensability pathway).

filing_deadline_notes: Benefits capped at 52 weeks from diagnosis date. No benefits beyond 4 years from qualifying event.
notes: Volunteer EMS and volunteer firefighters explicitly covered. Same qualifying event criteria as salaried firefighters. Not a traditional rebuttable presumption — compensability pathway requiring diagnosis + qualifying event.

filing_deadline_notes: Benefits capped at 52 weeks from diagnosis date. No benefits beyond 4 years from qualifying event.
notes: EMS personnel explicitly named under the firefighter definition in this section. Same qualifying event criteria apply.

filing_deadline_notes: Benefits capped at 52 weeks from diagnosis date. No benefits beyond 4 years from qualifying event.
notes: Law-enforcement officer definition is broad: State Police Officers' Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputy sheriffs; Dept. of Emergency Management hazmat officers; city sergeants of Richmond; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police officers; Capitol Police; VA ABC special agents; MWAA police; Norfolk Airport Authority police; Virginia Port Authority sworn officers; campus police (public institutions). Qualifying events (on/after July 1, 2020): serious bodily injury/death, incidents involving minors, immediate life threat, mass casualties, crime scene investigation. Not a traditional rebuttable presumption — compensability pathway requiring diagnosis by mental health professional + qualifying event criteria. Excludes PTSD from personnel actions.
